---
mode: agent
description: "Generate a Test Plan and Strategy for a D365 CE feature. Use when the user wants test planning documentation from an approved spec. Triggers on: 'test plan', 'test strategy', 'generate test plan', 'testing plan'."
---

Generate a Test Plan and Strategy for a D365 CE feature from an approved functional specification.

## Usage

```
/d365-ce-testplan {feature-name}
```

## Pre-condition Check

1. Read `specs/{feature-name}/review.md`.
2. If status is not `APPROVED`, stop: "Run /d365-ce-review first. Test planning requires an approved spec."

## Steps

3. Read `constitution/08-testing-standards.md`, `constitution/11-nfr-targets.md`, and `constitution/09-document-generation-rules.md`.
4. Read `specs/{feature-name}/spec.md` in full — extract: FR list, Business Rules, Actors/Personas, Security Roles, In/Out of Scope, module section headings.
5. Read `plans/{feature-name}/plan.md` if it exists — extract User Story references (US-NNN) and build the FR → US mapping. If the plan does not exist yet, set all US-NNN fields to `*(pending — run /d365-ce-plan first)*`.
6. Derive module codes from the spec's module section headings or plan Epic names (2–4 uppercase letters, e.g., `ACT` for Account, `PLG` for Plugin, `NOT` for Notifications, `SEC` for Security). If no module breakdown exists, use `CORE` as the single module code.
7. Generate all test case cards — one complete set per suite — before writing any file.
   Group cards into four suites: System/Integration, UAT, Security, Regression.
   Apply the card format from `doc-templates/test-case-suite-template.md` for each card.

8. Write the per-suite test case files using `doc-templates/test-case-suite-template.md`:
   - `docs-generated/{feature-name}/test-cases/system-integration.md` — System and Integration test cases
   - `docs-generated/{feature-name}/test-cases/uat.md` — UAT test cases (Business User assigned)
   - `docs-generated/{feature-name}/test-cases/security.md` — Security test cases (one card per security role)
   - `docs-generated/{feature-name}/test-cases/regression.md` — Regression test cases
   Each file starts with a Suite Summary table, then the full test case cards.

9. Write the main plan document using `doc-templates/test-plan-template.md`:
   - `docs-generated/{feature-name}/test-plan-and-strategy.md`
   - §5 contains reference tables only (TC ID, ALM ID, Title, Priority, Mapped FR, link column) — no full cards.
   - Each §5 subsection includes the relative file link, e.g., `[test-cases/system-integration.md](test-cases/system-integration.md)`.
   - Populate the Traceability Matrix (§12) and Test Suite Structure (§2) from the generated test cases.

10. Print summary:
   ```
   TEST PLAN COMPLETE — {feature-name}
   ════════════════════════════════════
   Modules  : {list of module codes}
   Cases    : {total}  (P1:{n}  P2:{n}  P3:{n})
   FRs      : {n} requirements covered
   US refs  : {n} linked  /  {n} pending (run /d365-ce-plan first)
   Files generated:
     docs-generated/{feature-name}/test-plan-and-strategy.md
     docs-generated/{feature-name}/test-cases/system-integration.md  ({n} cases)
     docs-generated/{feature-name}/test-cases/uat.md                 ({n} cases)
     docs-generated/{feature-name}/test-cases/security.md            ({n} cases)
     docs-generated/{feature-name}/test-cases/regression.md          ({n} cases)
   ```

## What the Test Plan Must Cover

### Test Strategy
- Approach: risk-based testing prioritised by business impact
- Scope: functional testing derived from the specification only — unit tests (Plugin/JS/PCF) are generated by `/d365-ce-implement` alongside the code and are not part of this document
- Test levels: System → Integration → UAT → Security → Regression
- Entry and exit criteria for each level

### Test Scope
- In scope: list each FR-NNN and the test type covering it
- Out of scope: what will not be tested and why
- Assumptions and constraints (e.g., test data availability, environment access)

### Test Types for D365 CE (functional scope only)

> Unit tests (Plugin FakeXrmEasy, JS Jest, PCF Jest) are **not** included here — they are generated by `/d365-ce-implement` when each component is built.

| Type | What to Test | Tool / Method |
|---|---|---|
| System Tests | Forms, views, Business Rules end-to-end in D365 CE | Manual + EasyRepro |
| Integration Tests | Plugin + Dataverse + external system | Test environment |
| UAT | All FR-NNN scenarios verified by business users | UAT environment |
| Security Tests | Role-based access per security matrix | Manual per persona |
| Regression | Existing features not broken by this change | Regression suite |
| Performance Tests | Response time against NFR targets in `11-nfr-targets.md` | EasyRepro / Azure Load Testing |

### Test Case IDs and Module Codes
- Format: `TC-{MODULE}-{NNN}` (e.g., `TC-ACT-001`, `TC-PLG-003`, `TC-SEC-001`)
- MODULE is derived from spec module headings or plan Epic names (2–4 uppercase letters)
- Numbers are sequential within each module, starting at 001

### Priority Mapping (integer, ADO-aligned)
| Value | Scope |
|---|---|
| 1 (P1) | Core functionality, data integrity, security enforcement, integration |
| 2 (P2) | Secondary workflows, Business Rules, error handling, notifications |
| 3 (P3) | Cosmetic, reporting, non-critical views |
| 4 (P4) | Future state, deferred items |

### Test Cases — Card Format
For every FR-NNN in the spec, generate full card detail into the per-suite files (`docs-generated/{feature}/test-cases/*.md`).
The main test plan document (`test-plan-and-strategy.md`) holds only the reference tables in §5 — not the full cards.
Use `doc-templates/test-case-suite-template.md` for the card structure:

```
### TC-{MODULE}-{NNN}: {Title}

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **TC ID** | TC-{MODULE}-{NNN} |
| **ALM ID** | *(pending — set by /d365-ce-alm sync)* |
| **Area Path** | {project}\{Feature}\{Module} |
| **Iteration Path** | {project}\Sprint {N} |
| **Priority** | {1 | 2 | 3 | 4} |
| **Assigned To** | {QA Engineer | Developer | Business User} |
| **State** | Design |
| **Automation** | Manual | Automated |
| **Test Type** | {Functional | Negative | Boundary | Integration | Security | Performance | UAT} |
| **Mapped FR** | FR-{NNN} |
| **User Story** | US-{NNN} |

**Preconditions:**
- {prerequisite 1}

**Test Steps:**
| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|-----------------|
| 1 | {action} | {expected result} |

**Post-conditions:**
- {what must be true after the test completes}

**Test Data:**
- {specific field values, entity names, or test data set reference}

**Notes:**
- {SLA targets from constitution/11-nfr-targets.md where applicable; cross-test dependencies}
```

Coverage requirements:
- Minimum 2 test cases per FR: 1 × Functional (happy path) + 1 × Negative
- Each Business Rule → 1 × Negative test verifying the rule fires
- Each Security Role → 1 × Security test verifying correct access
- Each integration touch-point → 1 × Integration test
- `ALM ID` always starts as `*(pending — set by /d365-ce-alm sync)*`
- `Assigned To`: QA Engineer for System/Integration/Security/Regression; Business User for UAT
- Test Steps: minimum 3 numbered rows with specific actions and measurable expected results

### Test Data Requirements
- List the test data needed per test scenario
- Identify sensitive/PII data and how it will be anonymised in test environments

### Test Environment Strategy
- Which environment for each test type (Dev / Test / UAT)
- Environment setup requirements specific to this feature

### Defect Management
- Defect severity levels: Critical / High / Medium / Low
- Definition per level in the context of D365 CE
- Exit criteria: no Critical/High open at UAT sign-off

### Roles and Responsibilities
| Role | Responsibility |
|---|---|
| QA Lead | Test plan ownership, defect triage, sign-off authority |
| Developer | Unit tests, defect resolution, test environment setup |
| QA Engineer | Integration, system, security, regression test execution |
| Functional Analyst | UAT coordination, test data preparation, AC validation |
| Business User | UAT execution, business process validation, sign-off |
| Release Manager | Regression gate, go/no-go decision |

### NFR Test Coverage
Read `constitution/11-nfr-targets.md` and populate the NFR Test Coverage section of the template with measurable acceptance criteria for each NFR category (Performance, Scalability, Security, Reliability, Usability).

### Traceability Matrix
Build FR → US → TC mapping as test cases are generated. Every FR-NNN must appear at least once. Output the completed matrix in §12 of the main test plan document. TC IDs in the matrix should link to their suite file (e.g., `[TC-ACT-001](test-cases/system-integration.md#tc-act-001-title)`).

### Test Suite Structure
After generating all test cases, count per module and populate the Test Suite Structure table (Section 2) with estimated counts and P1/P2/P3 distribution.
